paper_title: STARE: Surprisal-Guided Token-Level Advantage Reweighting for Policy Entropy Stability
original_problem: GRPO-style RLVR algorithms suffer from policy entropy collapse during LLM post-training — entropy decays rapidly, output diversity vanishes, policy converges prematurely, limiting long-horizon RL potential.

reconstructed_problem.root_cause: Token-level credit assignment mismatch in GRPO: trajectory-level advantages are shared across all tokens in a rollout, but per-token entropy contributions vary dramatically based on next-token surprisal. Low-surprisal tokens (high-probability, frequent) dominate updates and drive entropy down, while rare high-surprisal tokens that could maintain entropy are diluted.
reconstructed_problem.mechanism: GRPO's gradient update at a position where token a is sampled: Δz_v = η Â (δ_va - π_v). This pushes logits toward the sampled token a. Low-surprisal tokens are sampled more often → more frequent updates pushing toward them → distribution concentrates → entropy drops → self-reinforcing collapse loop.
reconstructed_problem.why_existing_solutions_fail: Existing methods operate at trajectory or sample granularity, ignoring that the collapse is fundamentally token-level. Clipping adjustments (DAPO) are inactive in on-policy regime. Trajectory-level weighting is too coarse. Entropy regularization over-amplifies and oscillates.

key_insights.insight: Per-token entropy variation = Â × Φ(p) — decomposes into advantage (shared across trajectory) × entropy sensitivity (token-local)
key_insights.implication: Reveals that entropy collapse is driven by token-level imbalance, not trajectory-level factors. Opens door for token-level intervention.

key_insights.insight: Four-quadrant structure: advantage × surprisal produces quadrants with unambiguous entropy direction
key_insights.implication: Identifies exactly which tokens to target: the 'diluted minority' of high-surprisal tokens in positive-advantage trajectories (L⁺) that could raise entropy but are overwhelmed by low-surprisal tokens.

key_insights.insight: Near-criticality: a tiny weight perturbation (W* ≈ 1 + O(1/T)) flips entropy direction
key_insights.implication: Intervention can be minimal — don't need strong regularization, just slight rebalancing of the credit assignment. Explains why STARE works with small W values (1.01-1.5).

key_insights.insight: Closed-loop target-entropy gating disengages when entropy is healthy
key_insights.implication: Minimally invasive: only intervenes when needed, reverts to standard GRPO otherwise. Makes the method robust to hyperparameter choice.

solution_architecture.name: STARE

components.operation: Select top-P% tokens by surprisal as entropy-critical subset
components.output: Token subsets L_q⁺, L_q⁻, U_q⁺, U_q⁻

components.operation: Scale effective advantage by W (>1) for selected subset (default: amplify L_q⁺)
components.output: Reweighted advantages inside GRPO clipped surrogate

components.operation: If H̄_k < H_tgt: activate reweighting; else: use standard GRPO

solution_architecture.interface: Drop-in modification to GRPO's advantage term inside the clipped surrogate objective

what_problem_remains: Theoretical analysis assumes unclipped regime (ρ ≈ 1); clipped dynamics not formally covered
Only applies to GRPO with shared group-normalized advantages — not PPO or REINFORCE
H_tgt (target entropy) is a manually set hyperparameter with no principled selection method
Computational overhead of per-token surprisal computation and quantile sorting not characterized
Limited to RLVR (math/code reasoning); general NL tasks unexplored
Token selection via quantile proxy may fail on edge-case surprisal distributions
No formal stability guarantees for the closed-loop target-entropy gating

meta_analysis.methodological_approach: Theory-first: derive token-level entropy dynamics → identify mismatch → design minimal intervention → validate across scales
meta_analysis.theoretical_depth: High — rigorous first-order gradient analysis with complete proofs in appendices (D-H). Novel application of information-theoretic concepts (surprisal) to RL optimization dynamics.
meta_analysis.empirical_rigor: Strong — multiple model scales (1.5B-32B), three task families, multiple baselines per setting, thorough ablations (8 STARE variants, hyperparameter sensitivity, cognitive analysis).
meta_analysis.reproducibility: Good — open-source code on GitHub. However, training compute requirements not stated explicitly.
meta_analysis.significance: High — addresses a critical bottleneck in RLVR post-training that limits long-horizon RL. The theoretical framework (four-quadrant, near-criticality) provides a principled foundation for future work.
